# The Mold Report

> Independent editorial coverage of mold illness, water-damaged buildings, biotoxin exposure, Chronic Inflammatory Response Syndrome (CIRS), indoor air quality, mycotoxins, and Shoemaker Protocol research.

The Mold Report is built and operated by Julien Lepleux. Articles are selected, rewritten, and compliance-checked by an editorial pipeline. Original reporting is always linked to the source. Each article page has a Markdown companion at the same path with a .md suffix (e.g. /a/{id}.md) that LLM crawlers can fetch as clean text.

## Site
- [Homepage](https://themoldreport.org/): Latest mold illness coverage
- [About](https://themoldreport.org/about.html): Editorial policy, ownership, disclosures
- [Mold 101](https://themoldreport.org/mold-101.html): Primer on mold illness, CIRS, and biotoxin exposure
- [Providers](https://themoldreport.org/providers.html): Provider directory
- [Research](https://themoldreport.org/research.html): Research and improvement evidence
- [Privacy](https://themoldreport.org/privacy.html)
- [Terms](https://themoldreport.org/terms.html)

## Full content
- [llms-full.txt](https://themoldreport.org/llms-full.txt): Concatenated full-text of every article on the site, suitable for one-shot ingestion.

## News
- [Military Families Sound Alarm Over Toxic Mold in Base Housing](https://themoldreport.org/a/03a74f2154ce.md) — 2026-04-30 — Service members and dependents say toxic mold in privatized military housing keeps causing respiratory and neurological symptoms.
- [Hinesville Mother Diagnosed with Respiratory Illness Amid Apartment Mold Complaints](https://themoldreport.org/a/93bf78649d51.md) — 2026-04-30 — A Hinesville, Georgia tenant says apartment mold caused a respiratory illness diagnosis. Management has now offered a fix after public complaints.
- [Miami University Students Report Black Mold in Dorm Showers and AC Units](https://themoldreport.org/a/036242ec9786.md) — 2026-04-27 — Students at Miami University detail black mold in Hahne and McKee Hall — including a moldy AC unit linked to two months of illness.
- [Flood Cleanup Safety: Why Water-Damaged Homes May Need Remediation Before Re-Entry](https://themoldreport.org/a/78af5ae1459e.md) — 2026-04-27 — Flood-damaged homes may need full remediation, not just drying, before they're safe to occupy. What the latest guidance says about mold risk.
- [Health Influencer Links Husband's 50-Pound Weight Loss to Black Mold at Home](https://themoldreport.org/a/d7a1e5baf969.md) — 2026-04-26 — A health influencer says her husband lost 50 pounds in under four months before traces of black mold in their home led the family to leave the residence.
- [Oxford parents demand transparency after mold found at Quaker Farms School](https://themoldreport.org/a/0a1327753916.md) — 2026-04-26 — Quaker Farms School families want clearer communication after mold found in classrooms; children report headaches, dizziness, and stomach pain.
- [Walt Disney World Removes Black Mold from 'It's a Small World' After Mass Visitor Exposure](https://themoldreport.org/a/9e9448a9f260.md) — 2026-04-25 — Walt Disney World has remediated black mold on its 'It's a Small World' ride after thousands of guests were likely exposed in Florida.
- [Reality Star's CIRS Diagnosis Puts Mold Illness in the National Spotlight](https://themoldreport.org/a/35b943ce02eb.md) — 2026-04-24 — Mikayla Matthews shares her battle with CIRS symptoms from suspected mold exposure, highlighting the link between water-damaged buildings and chronic illness.
- [Air Force Veteran Calls for Flood Relief as Water-Damaged Homes Breed Mold](https://themoldreport.org/a/6094ec896cde.md) — 2026-04-24 — Air Force veteran urges flood relief action as water-damaged homes breed mold, creating compounding health risks for families with prolonged exposure.
- [Georgia Tenant Living in Tent Amid Dispute Over Mold-Affected Rental](https://themoldreport.org/a/f7003aaca06c.md) — 2026-04-24 — A Georgia tenant says she has been forced to live in a tent after persistent mold problems in her rental home went unresolved. She has filed claims alleging her landlord did not remediate the property.
- [ASU Students Report Persistent Illness After Mold Exposure in Campus Dorms](https://themoldreport.org/a/39880a6c6c2a.md) — 2026-04-24 — Arizona State students report brain fog, fatigue, and chronic illness from mold in dorms. Symptoms resolved after leaving affected buildings.
- [Wisconsin Doctor Warns Flood Victims: Mold Growth Begins Within Days](https://themoldreport.org/a/ab63327c2468.md) — 2026-04-24 — ThedaCare physician warns Wisconsin flood victims that mold develops within days in water-damaged homes, with health effects that may appear weeks later.
- [People Magazine Covers Reality Star's Battle With Mold-Related Chronic Illness](https://themoldreport.org/a/054177519d78.md) — 2026-04-24 — People magazine covers reality star's mold-related chronic illness, bringing mainstream attention to biotoxin exposure and water-damaged building health risks.
- [Trade Medical Publication Frames Mold Toxicity as 'Expanding Clinical Frontier'](https://themoldreport.org/a/e9dfc3f29309.md) — 2026-04-20 — A medical trade publication frames mold toxicity and CIRS as a serious clinical concern, citing HLA-DR, C4a, TGF-β1, and MMP-9.

## Research
- [Virginia Tech Researchers Track Long-Term Mold Health Risks From Helene Flooding](https://themoldreport.org/a/17157d2a984c.md) — 2026-04-30 — Virginia Tech researchers say mold-related respiratory illness from Helene flooding typically appears one to three months after the water recedes.
- [Review Examines How the Mycotoxin Deoxynivalenol Damages the Liver](https://themoldreport.org/a/cf5df03af810.md) — 2026-04-28 — Archives of Toxicology review summarizes how the Fusarium trichothecene mycotoxin deoxynivalenol damages the liver, plus emerging protective strategies.
- [Long-Term Study Links Early Childhood Mold Exposure to Reduced Lung Function](https://themoldreport.org/a/f77398ac385d.md) — 2026-04-24 — New long-term research links indoor mold exposure in early childhood to reduced lung function, reinforcing concerns about water-damaged buildings and health.
- [Infectious Disease Specialists Address Challenges in Treating Invasive Mold Infections](https://themoldreport.org/a/92dc4f763d3a.md) — 2026-04-24 — Specialists review treatment challenges for invasive aspergillosis and mucormycosis, highlighting mold's serious health impacts beyond allergic reactions.
- [Mold Illness Highlighted Among Environmental Factors in Depression Research](https://themoldreport.org/a/6305216af437.md) — 2026-04-24 — New research review highlights mold illness and environmental toxicity as overlooked factors in depression, especially for patients in water-damaged buildings.
- [New Study: MARCoNS Bacteria Directly Suppress Alpha-MSH, Confirming Key Step in Shoemaker's Biotoxin Pathway](https://themoldreport.org/a/0d71278385f7.md) — 2026-04-22
- [Mold Species Identified Through Volatile Compound Analysis](https://themoldreport.org/a/4f6462b3ce58.md) — 2026-04-18 — 2025 study reveals consistent volatile biomarkers for Aspergillus versicolor identification across building materials like pine and gypsum.
- [Novel Biomarkers Help Assess Mold Exposure in Asthmatic Children](https://themoldreport.org/a/4f57d9fad231.md) — 2026-04-18 — 2025 study reveals how combined biomarkers help assess mold exposure in asthmatic children, improving clinical diagnosis and patient care.
- [Mold Volatile Compounds Trigger Toxicity in Office Building](https://themoldreport.org/a/fe754ecd550b.md) — 2026-04-18 — Case report on mold volatile organic compounds causing health symptoms in office buildings. Explore VOC toxicity beyond spores and mycotoxins.
- [Indoor Mold and Mycotoxins: Health Risks Explained](https://themoldreport.org/a/b0faf593934a.md) — 2026-04-18 — 2025 review of indoor mold, mycotoxins, and bacteria in buildings. Learn about toxic fungal species and health risks for occupants.
- [Researchers Identify Nitrogen and Carbon Sources That Boost Mycotoxin Production in Mold](https://themoldreport.org/a/ae8317d213fc.md) — 2026-04-18 — Research reveals how nitrogen and carbon sources impact mycotoxin production in Stachybotrys chartarum mold and trichothecene biosynthesis.
- [Inhaled Black Mold Toxicity Study in Mice Reveals Health Effects](https://themoldreport.org/a/7b761cfcb022.md) — 2026-04-18 — National Toxicology Program study reveals health effects of inhaled Stachybotrys chartarum in mice. Key findings on black mold toxicity.
- [Aspergillus Species Pose Growing Health Risks, Study Finds](https://themoldreport.org/a/9d03b2bbfe76.md) — 2026-04-18 — Research reveals growing health risks from Aspergillus fumigatus, flavus, and niger. Study examines disease mechanisms and antifungal resistance patterns.
- [Mold in Australian Homes Linked to Health and Social Problems](https://themoldreport.org/a/4977bfb7b6f7.md) — 2026-04-18 — Australian study reveals how mold exposure affects health, daily functioning, and quality of life in residential homes. Research findings by Gatto, Li, and Bentley.
- [Biomarkers Distinguish ME/CFS From Chronic Inflammatory Response Syndrome](https://themoldreport.org/a/52e57df145ca.md) — 2026-04-18 — Study reveals biomarkers distinguishing ME/CFS from Chronic Inflammatory Response Syndrome using contrast sensitivity and transcriptomic analysis.
- [Mold and Dampness Linked to Fatigue, Study Review Shows](https://themoldreport.org/a/00639faab62a.md) — 2026-04-18 — Study review reveals consistent associations between mold exposure and damp indoor environments with fatigue symptoms in peer-reviewed research.
- [Homeowners Combat Mold With Microbiome Engineering](https://themoldreport.org/a/77c239915579.md) — 2026-04-18 — Explore how homeowners combat mold using microbiome engineering. Research insights on household mold mitigation strategies.
- [Hospitals Combat Mold Infestations to Protect Patient Safety](https://themoldreport.org/a/90199dfcbc42.md) — 2026-04-18 — Learn how hospitals identify, manage, and prevent mold infestations to protect vulnerable patients from fungal exposure risks.
- [Chronic Inflammatory Response Syndrome: Evidence for Treatment Efficacy](https://themoldreport.org/a/7c20d4d71ec0.md) — 2026-04-18 — Peer-reviewed research on Chronic Inflammatory Response Syndrome treatment efficacy, examining mold and biotoxin exposure connections.
- [Review Maps Widespread Mycotoxin Contamination Across Central Africa's Food and Feed Supply](https://themoldreport.org/a/79441f59eb92.md) — 2026-04-10 — A new review documents widespread aflatoxin, fumonisin, and ochratoxin A contamination in Central African food and feed, with major surveillance gaps.
- [Greek Researchers Detect Ochratoxin A Across Pig Tissues, Highlighting Mycotoxin Reach in Food Supply](https://themoldreport.org/a/12c3b7de5eb7.md) — 2026-04-08 — Greek researchers measured ochratoxin A across kidney, liver, muscle and fat in pigs, raising new questions about dietary mycotoxin exposure.
- [Two Kidney-Damaging Mold Toxins Actually Cancel Each Other Out, Study Finds](https://themoldreport.org/a/4120eb485fb9.md) — 2026-04-08 — New research finds mold toxins ochratoxin A and citrinin neutralize each other's kidney damage when combined. See what 3D renal microsphere testing revealed.
- [Common Airborne Mold May Speed Lung Cancer Growth, Lab Study Finds](https://themoldreport.org/a/459ccc5eda3b.md) — 2026-04-07 — A new lab study finds Aspergillus fumigatus may promote tumor-friendly conditions in lung cancer cells. Learn what airborne mold exposure could mean for cancer risk.
- [Dr. Ritchie Shoemaker to Host AMA on r/ToxicMoldExposure, Marking 25 Years of Mold Illness Research](https://themoldreport.org/a/4832fa4a3139.md) — 2026-04-05 — Dr. Ritchie Shoemaker joins Reddit's r/ToxicMoldExposure for a live AMA marking 25 years since he first linked biotoxin exposure to CIRS. See what to ask.
- [Human Contact Frequency as a Dominant Ecological Driver of Fungal Community Assembly and Homogenization in Public Built Environments.](https://themoldreport.org/a/93dccf1b2f86.md) — 2026-04-03 — New research finds high-contact surfaces in public buildings harbor homogenized mold communities. See how human touch frequency drives fungal spread across locations.
- [Two Common Grain Mold Toxins Damage Brain Cells More When Combined, Study Finds](https://themoldreport.org/a/bba0d96d84f3.md) — 2026-04-02 — Combined exposure to grain mold toxins DON and fumonisin B1 causes greater brain cell damage than either alone, per new Journal of Hazardous Materials research.
- [What Lives in Your Home's Air? New Review Maps Indoor Microbiome Science](https://themoldreport.org/a/f0e9ccd067f9.md) — 2026-04-01 — A new review in Environmental Microbiology Reports maps how scientists sample and analyze mold, bacteria, and fungi living in indoor air and on home surfaces.
- [Indoor Mold Exposure Tied to Higher Childhood Asthma Risk, Augusta Takes Notice](https://themoldreport.org/a/6a96fe388474.md) — 2026-03-31 — New research links indoor mold exposure to higher asthma risk in children. See how Augusta, GA health officials are responding to findings on mold in homes and schools.
- [Indoor air bacterial and fungal burden in the environment of an atopic child: implications for elevated urine mycotoxin levels.](https://themoldreport.org/a/4c5cf73834bc.md) — 2026-03-01 — New research links indoor fungal air burden to elevated urine mycotoxin levels in a child with atopic disease. See what this means for mold-related health testing.
- [Mold Removal in Public Housing Cuts Asthma ER Visits, Study Finds](https://themoldreport.org/a/fe9aedc6c1da.md) — 2026-02-18 — A 2026 study found targeted mold removal in public housing measurably reduced asthma-related ER visits. See what the findings mean for housing health policy.

## Regulation
- [Spring Hill College responds to alumna's mold-and-asthma lawsuit](https://themoldreport.org/a/32d22be4746e.md) — 2026-04-26 — Alabama college issues statement after alumna sues over alleged campus mold exposure that she says caused severe asthma and respiratory issues.
- [Auckland landlord ordered to pay $13,000 over moldy rental linked to tenant illness](https://themoldreport.org/a/1d8d11cae91c.md) — 2026-04-26 — NZ Tenancy Tribunal cites prolonged damp and mold exposure as cause of tenant's illness in $13,000 ruling against an Auckland landlord.

- [Uponor Faces Lawsuits Over Defective PEX Pipe Sales (2010-2021)](https://themoldreport.org/a/2928828c5dbc.md) — 2026-04-17 — Uponor faces lawsuits over defective AquaPEX pipes (2010-2021) causing moisture intrusion and mold growth in homes.

## Industry

- [Mold Remediation Market Approaches $4 Billion as Consolidation Accelerates](https://themoldreport.org/a/b5b0e7728f9c.md) — 2026-04-25 — The mold remediation market is on track to grow from $2.3B in 2025 to $3.9B by 2033, with consolidation among national restoration platforms.
- [Mycotoxin Testing Market Set to Nearly Double by 2034, Hitting $2.78 Billion](https://themoldreport.org/a/ac44f44af113.md) — 2026-04-25 — The mycotoxin testing market is forecast to hit $2.78B by 2034, but urine panels lack validated ranges and can't distinguish food from building exposure.

## Diagnostics
- [MoldCo Drops Mold Blood Test Price From $99 to $56](https://themoldreport.org/a/d972861abbe1.md) — 2026-04-12 — MoldCo cut its Starter Health Panel from $99 to $56, screening TGF-beta1, MMP-9, and MSH mold illness biomarkers at 2,000+ LabCorp locations nationwide.
- [MoldCo Drops Barriers to Mold Illness Care With Virtual Clinic Model](https://themoldreport.org/a/7800e6f545a6.md) — 2026-01-14 — MoldCo's telehealth model gives mold illness patients access to real clinicians, biomarker testing, and treatment — no specialist waitlists or $10K intake fees.
